Title: A cell-type-specific regulon controlling monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis with feedback and feedforward activation loops

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically important specialized metabolites; anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: late-stage monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis in idioblast cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates DAT; D4H; THAS2; with CrIDB1/CaTT8 additionally activates T16H2 and 16OMT and activates downstream TFs CrIDM4, CrIDM3, CrIDW1, and CrIDB1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DAT; D4H; THAS2; CrIDB1; CrIDM4; CrIDM3; CrIDW1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions with bHLH partner CrIDB1 or CaTT8 in combinatorial activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in feedforward loop by activating CrIDM4 together with CrIDB1; participates in feedback loop enabling CrIDB1 self-activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: idioblast-specific in C. roseus based on prior study cited by authors
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: idioblast-specific regulator of leaf metabolic regulon; tested in petals by transient overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: methyl jasmonate responsive idioblast regulon context
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: single-cell RNA-seq, single-cell ATAC-seq, petal RNA-seq overexpression, BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrIDM1 overexpression activated late-stage vinca alkaloid genes DAT, D4H, and THAS2. 2. Co-overexpression with CrIDB1 or CaTT8 strengthened activation and induced additional upstream genes and TFs. 3. CrIDM1 sits high in the idioblast TF hierarchy and contributes to feedforward and feedback activation loops.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient overexpression in C. roseus petals; RNA-seq differential expression; integration with single-cell coexpression and prior TF-promoter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate induces idioblast TF network in prior expression data
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major upstream idioblast activator for late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator for boosting late-stage MIA production, likely best used with CrIDB1 or other idioblast TFs for stronger pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog of CaMYB123, but unlike CaMYB123 showed clear activation in heterologous petal assay.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically important specialized metabolites; anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: late-stage monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis in idioblast cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: upregulates late-stage biosynthetic genes and activates CrIDM2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes; CrIDM2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: acts in feedforward loop downstream of CrIDM1+CrIDB1 and activates overlapping late-stage biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly idioblast-specific in leaf; enriched in pigmented and idioblast cells in petals; highly expressed in sorted idioblast fraction and absent from mesophyll
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species; tested in petals by transient overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: activated by CrIDM1 + CrIDB1/CaTT8; weakly induced by methyl jasmonate (log2FC 0.75)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: single-cell RNA-seq, sorted-cell RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, petal RNA-seq overexpression, BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrIDM4 is an idioblast-enriched MYB identified from orthology with CaMYB5. 2. Overexpression alone activated late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes. 3. It is activated downstream of CrIDM1+CrIDB1, creating a feedforward loop in the idioblast regulon.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-cell expression analysis; sorted-cell expression; transient overexpression in C. roseus petals with RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate weak induction reported from prior dataset
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: idioblast-specific activator of late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful regulatory gene to enhance idioblast-specific late-stage MIA pathway, potentially stack with CrIDM1/CrIDB1
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: locus shows highly accessible chromatin in idioblast
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named in this study based on idioblast specificity and orthology to CaMYB5.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically important specialized metabolites; anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: with CrIDM1 strongly activates late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes and reinforces its own expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: idioblast-specific activation of late-stage monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation in combination with MYB partner; autoregulatory feedback with MYB-dependent promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: alone no clear activation; with CrIDM1 activates DAT, D4H, THAS2, T16H2, 16OMT, and TFs including CrIDM4; activates CrIDB1 promoter with CrIDM1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DAT; D4H; THAS2; T16H2; 16OMT; CrIDM4; CrIDB1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: feedback loop via self-activation with CrIDM1; feedforward loop by activating CrIDM4 with CrIDM1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: idioblast-specific in leaf; enriched in pigmented and idioblast cells in petals; highly expressed in sorted idioblast fraction and absent from mesophyll; methyl jasmonate inducible
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species; functional assay in petals and promoter assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for petal overexpression; UBQ10 for overexpression in reporter assay; native CrIDB1 promoter fragment in reporter construct
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: single-cell RNA-seq, sorted-cell RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, petal RNA-seq overexpression, reporter assay; sequencing BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrIDB1 is an idioblast-specific TT8-clade bHLH discovered via orthology to CaTT8. 2. Alone it did not activate pathway genes, but with CrIDM1 it strongly activated late-stage vinca alkaloid genes and induced CrIDM4. 3. CrIDB1 and CrIDM1 activated the CrIDB1 promoter, establishing a positive feedback loop.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-cell and sorted-cell expression analysis; transient co-overexpression in petals with RNA-seq; promoter transactivation assay in N. benthamiana; ATAC-seq-guided promoter selection
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate induction; reporter assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent coactivator essential for strong idioblast late-stage pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable combinatorial regulator for engineering idioblast-specific MIA production, should be paired with CrIDM1 rather than used alone
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CrIDB1 locus has idioblast-accessible chromatin; promoter accessibility likely contributes to cell-type specificity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog of CaTT8; expression and promoter accessibility are idioblast-specific.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: early monoterpene indole alkaloid pathway intermediates supplying vinca alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: monoterpene indole alkaloid / iridoid precursor pathway
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supplies precursors for downstream pharmacologically active MIAs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates early biosynthetic genes in the MEP and iridoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates methyl-D-erythritol phosphate pathway and iridoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MEP pathway genes; iridoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: methyl jasmonate inducible according to cited prior work
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested by transient overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: petal RNA-seq overexpression; prior expression data
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrBIS1 overexpression activated early MIA pathway genes. 2. Its effect was limited to MEP and iridoid pathway steps, not late-stage vinca alkaloid genes. 3. This matches previous characterization of BIS TFs as regulators of iridoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in C. roseus petals with RNA-seq differential expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major activator of upstream precursor pathways feeding MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for increasing precursor flux into MIAs, likely to be stacked with late-stage regulators for full pathway enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as known regulator and benchmark; ortholog of CaBIS, which was inactive in this heterologous assay.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: vinca alkaloids in assay species; likely camptothecin-related MIA pathway in source species
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: idioblast in C. roseus; rare MIA-producing stem cell clusters in C. acuminata
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically important specialized metabolites; anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes when overexpressed in C. roseus, suggesting conserved regulator activity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: late-stage MIA biosynthesis in rare biosynthetic cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates overlapping target gene set with CrIDM4; also activates CrIDM2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes; CrIDM2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: orthologous regulator linked to feedforward module represented in C. roseus by CrIDM4
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with MIA biosynthetic genes in Cluster 17 of C. acuminata stem rare biosynthetic cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested by transient overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: C. acuminata stem single-cell RNA-seq; C. roseus petal RNA-seq overexpression; BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaMYB5 was identified as a MYB coexpressed with MIA genes in rare C. acuminata stem cells. 2. Overexpression in C. roseus activated late-stage vinca alkaloid genes, similar to CrIDM4. 3. This supports conserved cell-type-specific regulon logic across Asterid species.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-cell coexpression analysis in C. acuminata; heterologous transient overexpression in C. roseus petals with RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: conserved orthologous activator candidate for rare-cell MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising cross-species regulator for metabolic engineering of late-stage MIA production
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most closely related C. acuminata homolog to CrIDM4 according to phylogeny.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: vinca alkaloids in assay species; likely camptothecin-related MIA pathway in source species
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: idioblast in C. roseus; rare MIA-expressing stem cell clusters in C. acuminata
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically important specialized metabolites; anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: with CrIDM1 activates late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes more strongly than CrIDM1 alone; alone inactive
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: combinatorial activation of late-stage MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: with CrIDM1 activates DAT, D4H, THAS2, T16H2, 16OMT and TFs including CrIDB1 and CrIDM4
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DAT; D4H; THAS2; T16H2; 16OMT; CrIDB1; CrIDM4
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: contributes to the same feedback/feedforward logic observed for its ortholog CrIDB1 in heterologous system
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with MIA biosynthetic genes in Clusters 14 and 17 of C. acuminata stem
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested by transient overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: requires MYB partner CrIDM1 for activity in assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: C. acuminata stem single-cell RNA-seq; C. roseus petal RNA-seq overexpression; BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaTT8 was identified as a TT8-clade bHLH coexpressed with MIA genes in C. acuminata stem. 2. Alone it did not activate pathway genes in C. roseus petals. 3. In combination with CrIDM1 it strongly activated late-stage vinca alkaloid genes and idioblast TFs, indicating conserved coactivator function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-cell coexpression analysis in C. acuminata; heterologous transient co-overexpression in C. roseus petals with RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent coactivator for strong late-stage MIA activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: should be deployed with compatible MYB partner such as CrIDM1 in engineering designs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Representative member selected from multiple equally related homologs by expression level and breadth.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: vinca alkaloid precursor pathway in assay species; likely camptothecin precursor pathway in source species
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: monoterpene indole alkaloid / iridoid precursor pathway
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rare biosynthetic stem cells in source species
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: no clear activation of early MIA genes in C. roseus petal assay despite orthology to CrBIS1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: putative precursor pathway regulation in source species
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional activation, not supported in heterologous assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: NA in C. roseus petal assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: single bHLH TF in Cluster 21, coexpressed with iridoid/MIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested by transient overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: C. acuminata stem single-cell RNA-seq; C. roseus petal RNA-seq overexpression; BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaBIS was identified as a BIS-clade bHLH coexpressed with MIA genes in C. acuminata rare cells. 2. Unlike CrBIS1, CaBIS did not activate early pathway genes in C. roseus petals. 3. Its native function in C. acuminata remains likely but unproven in planta due to lack of transient assay system.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-cell coexpression analysis; heterologous transient overexpression in C. roseus petals with RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: not established in applied species; candidate regulator in source species
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: requires native-species validation before engineering use
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog of CrBIS1 but displayed divergent activity in heterologous assay.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: vinca alkaloids in assay species; likely camptothecin-related MIA pathway in source species
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rare MIA-expressing stem cells in source species
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically important specialized metabolites; anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: did not activate late-stage vinca alkaloid biosynthetic genes in C. roseus petal assay
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: putative regulation of MIA biosynthesis in source species, unsupported in assay species
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional activation, not validated in heterologous assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: NA in C. roseus petal assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: tested with CaTT8/CrIDB1 but did not support activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with MIA biosynthetic genes in Cluster 17 of C. acuminata stem
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested by transient overexpression in petals
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: C. acuminata stem single-cell RNA-seq; C. roseus petal RNA-seq overexpression; BioProject PRJNA1243444
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaMYB123 is a C. acuminata MYB coexpressed with MIA genes. 2. Unlike its proposed C. roseus ortholog CrIDM1, it did not activate late-stage vinca alkaloid genes in heterologous petal assays. 3. This indicates functional divergence or assay/context dependence.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-cell coexpression analysis; transient overexpression alone and in combinations in C. roseus petals with RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: currently weak candidate until validated in C. acuminata
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Representative member selected from MYB123 clade based on expression; proposed orthology to CrIDM1 is functional but not one-to-one proven.
